basic_details.job_title: Amethi BC Sakhi Recruitment 2026 - 166 Gram Panchayat Vacancies, Apply Before 30 June
basic_details.organization_name: Office of Deputy Commissioner Self Employment, Amethi
basic_details.short_information: The Office of the Deputy Commissioner Self Employment, District Amethi, Uttar Pradesh has invited applications from eligible women candidates for the post of Banking Correspondent Sakhi (BC Sakhi) in 166 vacant Gram Panchayats under Janpad Amethi. This recruitment is being conducted under Advertisement No. /N0AR0EL0M0/B0C0 Sakhi Chayan/2026-27 dated 06 June 2026. Interested and eligible women SHG members must submit their applications offline through their respective Self Help Group before the last date of 30 June 2026.

application_fee.details: No application fee has been mentioned in the official notification. The application process is offline and free of cost through the SHG channel.

age_limit.details: Age relaxation for reserved categories will be applicable as per Uttar Pradesh Government rules. Candidates should confirm details from the district office.

qualification.details: Who Can Apply? | Only women candidates fulfilling all of the following conditions are eligible: | Must be a member of a Self Help Group (SHG) and must not be a loan defaulter Age between 18 years and 40 years Minimum educational qualification: Class 12 (Intermediate) pass Must have knowledge of operating a mobile phone and internet Must be willing to accept a support fund of ₹75,000 from UPSRLM at 4% annual interest rate Must be a permanent resident of the same Gram Panchayat for which she is applying Must be willing to travel throughout the Gram Panchayat, visit households, and go to business establishments for work | minimum: Class 12 (Intermediate) pass from a recognised board | preferred: Knowledge of banking and digital financial operations

salary_details.details: BC Sakhi is not a fixed salaried government post. Income is mainly commission-based from banking transactions conducted at the Gram Panchayat level. | Support fund of ₹75,000 from UPSRLM at 4% annual interest rate Amount can be used to purchase Micro-ATM, handheld device, or other required equipment Commission income depends on transaction volume The ₹75,000 support fund is a loan, not a grant

selection_process: Stage 2 Gram Sangathan and Sankul Sthariya Sangh verification
how_to_apply: Interested women SHG members should apply through their own Self Help Group.
Application forms can be collected from the SHG, Gram Sangathan, or Sankul Sthariya Sangh.
After SHG-level shortlisting, the Gram Sangathan forwards applications to the Sankul Sthariya Sangh.
The Sankul Sthariya Sangh prepares a consolidated list of endorsed BC Sakhi candidates.
The list is sent to the District Mission Management Unit (DMMU), Amethi through the Vikas Khand office.
Final selection is done through written test and interview by a committee headed by the Chief Development Officer.
Note: Candidates should not submit applications directly to the district office. Applications must be submitted through the SHG → Gram Sangathan → Sankul Sthariya Sangh → Vikas Khand → DMMU channel.
important_instructions: The vacant Gram Panchayat list is not included in the notification. Candidates must collect the vacancy list from the concerned Vikas Khand office. Applications submitted directly to the district office will not be considered. Candidates can apply only for their permanent resident Gram Panchayat. The advertisement is displayed at Vikas Khand offices across Amethi.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Amethi BC Sakhi 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application is 30 June 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in Amethi BC Sakhi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 166 Gram Panchayats have vacant BC Sakhi positions in Amethi district.

faq.question: Q3. Can I apply online for Amethi BC Sakhi?
faq.answer: No. The application process is entirely offline through the SHG → Gram Sangathan → Vikas Khand channel. No online application portal is available.

faq.question: Q4. What is the minimum qualification for BC Sakhi?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed Class 12 (Intermediate) from a recognised board.

faq.question: Q5. Is BC Sakhi a government job with fixed salary?
faq.answer: No. BC Sakhi is a commission-based role. Income depends on the number of banking transactions conducted. A support fund of ₹75,000 at 4% interest is provided by UPSRLM to set up operations.

faq.question: Q6. Can I apply for a Gram Panchayat outside my village?
faq.answer: No. Candidates must be a permanent resident of the same Gram Panchayat for which they are applying.

faq.question: Q7. Who conducts the final selection?
faq.answer: Final selection is done by a committee chaired by the Chief Development Officer (CDO), Amethi based on written test and interview scores.
